$comment: Cross-SDK test vectors for Danipa webhook HMAC-SHA256 signing. Every Danipa SDK (Node, Java, future PHP / Python / Go) MUST produce the exact `expectedSignature` for each `(payload, secret, timestamp)` triple. Drift here means a backend webhook will validate in one SDK but reject in another — treat any test failure as a release-blocker. Algorithm: signedContent = `${timestamp}.${payload}` (UTF-8); signature = `sha256=` + lowercase-hex HMAC-SHA256(secret, signedContent).

vectors.name: default-test-vector
vectors.description: The original Node-SDK fixture vector. Do not change without coordinating across all SDKs.

vectors.name: realistic-payment-event
vectors.description: Representative real-world webhook payload — collection completed event.

vectors.name: empty-payload
vectors.description: Edge case — empty body. SDK should still compute a deterministic signature.

vectors.name: unicode-payload-and-secret
vectors.description: Validates UTF-8 byte-encoding of both payload and secret. CJK + emoji in payload, multi-byte chars in secret.
